description: Unit: parsing watch list from null attributes returns empty array.
Unit: parsing watch list from legacy string email array normalizes into active entries.
Unit: parser normalizes mixed-case emails to lowercase.
Unit: duplicate watcher entries collapse to a single normalized email entry.
Unit: merge preserves optional metadata when available and non-empty.
Unit: merge adds new watcher entry with active=true by default.
Unit: merge reactivates existing inactive watcher when recipient is re-seen.
Unit: serialization removes `watch_list` key when resulting list is empty.
Unit: inbound recipient builder includes both To and CC entries with source tags.
Unit: inbound recipient builder excludes sender/provider mailbox/excluded addresses.
UI: ticket properties render Watch List card and existing watchers.
UI: adding a valid manual watcher triggers persistence callback and refreshes displayed list.
UI: adding invalid email shows validation error and does not persist.
UI: adding existing inactive watcher reactivates instead of creating duplicate row.
UI: unchecking watcher updates active=false and persists.
UI: checking inactive watcher updates active=true and persists.
UI: removing watcher deletes row and persists attribute update.
UI: watcher save controls disable while request is in-flight to prevent double submits.
Inbound unit: new ticket path includes watch-list attributes from To/CC recipients.
Inbound unit: new ticket watch-list seed excludes sender email.
Inbound unit: new ticket watch-list seed excludes provider mailbox.
Inbound unit: reply-token path calls watch-list upsert for existing ticket.
Inbound unit: thread-header path calls watch-list upsert for existing ticket.
Inbound unit: when no To/CC recipients remain after exclusions, no watch-list upsert is attempted.
DB integration (happy path): upsert watch-list recipients writes expected JSON in `tickets.attributes.watch_list`.
DB integration (guard case): upsert with only excluded/invalid recipients performs no ticket attribute mutation.
Subscriber unit: active watcher extraction ignores inactive entries.
Subscriber unit: ticket-created sends watcher notification with customer-visible context.
Subscriber unit: ticket-updated accumulator path includes watchers in accumulation set.
Subscriber unit: ticket-updated non-accumulator path sends watcher notifications immediately.
Subscriber unit: ticket-assigned sends watcher notifications once per unique recipient.
Subscriber unit: public internal-agent comment sends watcher notifications.
Subscriber unit: internal comment does not send watcher notifications.
Subscriber unit: public client-authored comment does not trigger watcher sends when customer-visible rule requires agent-authored updates.
Subscriber unit: ticket-closed sends watcher notifications with customer-visible context.
Subscriber unit: watcher recipients dedupe against primary contact/client recipient.
Subscriber unit: watcher recipients dedupe against assigned/additional resource recipients.
Subscriber unit: comment author is suppressed for watcher sends when author email matches watcher entry.
Subscriber unit: watcher send function issues one send call per watcher email (no aggregated CC payload).
End-to-end notification test: watcher receives ticket-created email as separate message.
End-to-end notification test: watcher receives ticket-updated email as separate message.
End-to-end notification test: watcher receives public comment email and not internal-note email.
End-to-end notification test: watcher receives ticket-closed email as separate message.
Compatibility test: technician/internal email can be added manually as watcher and remains persisted/active.
